subtraction.title: ➖ Subtraction
subtraction.html:
Subtraction means take away. When we subtract, we find out how many are left.

We use a minus sign (−) for subtraction. For example: 8 − 3 = 5. We say 'eight minus three equals five' or 'eight take away three is five'.

We can use columns to subtract big numbers. We write the numbers one under the other, in a column. We start subtracting from the right side.

A single-digit number has one digit, like 5 or 9. A double-digit number has two digits, like 15 or 42.

Subtraction is everywhere! If you have 10 sweets and you eat 3, how many are left? 10 − 3 = 7. You have 7 sweets left!

problems.title: 🧮 Subtraction Word Problems
problems.html:
Let's solve some word problems using subtraction!

Tom had 15 stickers. He gave 6 to his friend. How many stickers does Tom have now? 15 − 6 = 9. Tom has 9 stickers.

Sara bought 20 apples. Her family ate 8 apples. How many apples are left? 20 − 8 = 12. There are 12 apples left.

The class had 25 students. 7 students were absent. How many students came to school? 25 − 7 = 18. 18 students came to school.

In a test score, Max got 10 out of 10. He was clean — no mistakes! But his friend got 7 out of 10. He was a bit tired that day.

The key to subtraction is: start with the bigger number and take away the smaller number!
